title: Concrete, light item — quick recommendation
summary: For a light item in concrete, a standard expansion plug and a concrete drill bit are usually enough.
steps: Use a concrete drill bit and a plug matching the screw size.
warnings: Check for hidden cables or pipes before drilling.

title: Concrete, light item — full guide
summary: Use the right hole diameter, remove dust and tighten carefully.
steps: Mark the point with a pencil.
Match the drill bit to the plug diameter.
Tighten the screw without excessive force.
dontDo: do not drill without checking installations
do not use an oversized drill bit

title: Concrete, medium weight — quick recommendation
summary: For medium weight in concrete, use a stronger plug and keep a safety margin.
steps: Choose a plug with spare load capacity.
warnings: Do not mount heavy items overhead without certainty.

title: Concrete, medium weight — full guide
summary: Use multiple points, proper plugs and careful drilling.
steps: Check the object weight and mounting points.
dontDo: do not mount if concrete crumbles

title: Brick — quick recommendation
summary: Brick can crumble, so choose the plug for the exact wall type.
steps: Drill without hammer mode if the brick crumbles.
Use a plug for solid or hollow brick.
warnings: Not every brick wall has the same load capacity.

title: Brick — full guide
summary: First decide if the brick is solid, hollow or block type.
steps: Choose a plug for solid or hollow masonry.
Reduce hammer mode if the wall crumbles.
Insert the plug and tighten carefully.
dontDo: do not drill too close to an edge

title: Drywall, light item — quick recommendation
summary: For light items on drywall, use a plug made for plasterboard.

title: Drywall, light item — full guide
summary: Use the correct drywall plug and avoid point overload.
steps: Check if there is a stud nearby.
dontDo: do not hang heavy items on board only
do not overtighten and damage the board

title: Wood — quick recommendation
summary: In wood, use a proper wood screw, often without a plug.
steps: Choose screw length for the thickness.
Make a pilot hole if needed.

title: Wood — full guide
summary: Choose screw length, diameter and drilling direction carefully.
steps: Make a pilot hole in thin wood.
dontDo: do not use a screw that is too short
do not screw too close to the edge

title: Heavy or risky mounting — be careful
summary: Do not guess with heavy items. You need certainty about wall and fixing capacity.
steps: Do not start without checking the wall.
warnings: Heavy items can fall and cause damage or injury.

title: Heavy or risky mounting — full checklist
summary: For heavy items, the app should not replace a professional assessment.
steps: Do not mount above beds, sofas or walkways without certainty.
Use a fixing system with spare capacity.
dontDo: do not use random plugs for heavy items

title: Fixing selection — quick help
summary: Start by identifying the wall type and object weight.
warnings: Do not mount heavy items without certainty.

title: Fixing selection — general checklist
summary: Safe mounting starts with wall type, weight, fixing points and proper hardware.
steps: Decide the number of fixing points.
Choose plug or screw for the material.
